values.value: Sends notifications via Salesforce's built-in Messaging.SingleEmailMessage. No HTTP callout required - uses org email deliverability. Each recipient carries the target email address (or points at a User record whose email is used). Subject to Salesforce daily email limits.

## Email setup
The Email notifier uses Salesforce's built-in outbound email. There's nothing to configure on the service itself - just create the service and add recipients.
### Requirements
1. **Email deliverability** must be enabled for the org: Setup -> **Deliverability** -> **Access Level** = `All email` 2. The **running user** must have `Send Email` enabled on their profile / permission set (for outbound messages triggered from anonymous Apex or sync contexts). Async notifier dispatch runs as the Automated Process user - make sure that user's profile permits outbound email 3. Each recipient must set either `Email Address` (raw email address) or `User` (lookup to a User whose email is used)
### Sender-domain compliance (DKIM / DMARC alignment)
Salesforce enforces DMARC alignment for outbound email as part of the industry-wide bulk-sender requirements rolled out with Winter '24 / Spring '25. A message whose `From` domain does not have a DKIM key registered in the org - and is not sent through an Org-Wide Email Address whose domain is properly aligned - is silently dropped by the outbound relay **even though `Messaging.sendEmail` returns `isSuccess = true`**. The delivery row will show `Delivered` because Salesforce accepted the API call; the mail server never accepted the actual message.
This is why:
- **Scratch orgs and freshly created Developer Edition orgs cannot reliably deliver email.** They have no DKIM keys and no Org-Wide Email Addresses by default. Even with Deliverability = `All email`, sends made from `<user>@salesforce.com`-style addresses are dropped at the relay. Test the Email notifier in a sandbox / DE org that has DKIM + an aligned Org-Wide Email Address configured, not a scratch org. - **Production orgs should configure both** before enabling this notifier for real recipients: 1. Setup -> **DKIM Keys** -> create + activate a DKIM key for the sending domain; publish the CNAME records Salesforce provides 2. Setup -> **Organization-Wide Email Addresses** -> add an OWEA whose domain matches the DKIM key, and grant it to the profile(s) that will run notifier dispatch (including Automated Process) 3. The recipient's `User` (or `Email Address`) is unrelated to the sender domain - the sender comes from the running user's email or the OWEA. Aligning the *sender* is the fix. - To troubleshoot a silently dropped send, use Setup -> **Email Log Files** -> **Request an Email Log**. Salesforce records the rejection reason (SPF / DKIM / DMARC failure) on rows that the API-level `SendEmailResult` reports as successful.
### Recipient configuration (JSON)
The recipient's `Configuration (JSON)` field controls what gets rendered in the email body and (optionally) the subject line.
- `sourceFields` - which source-record fields render in the body. Each entry is either a bare string (`"LoggingLevel__c"`) or an object (`{"field": "Log__c", "displayLabel": "Parent Log"}`). Object entries support `displayField` (a different field whose value is rendered) and `displayLabel` (a label override in place of the field's describe label). Dotted paths (`Log__r.LoggedBy__r.Name`) are supported and are hydrated via SOQL at delivery time. - `includeNullValues` - `true` renders blank fields as `(empty)`; the default `false` drops them. - `subjectTemplate` - custom subject line with `{!token}` merge tokens. Built-in tokens: `{!ruleName}`, `{!matchCount}`. Any other token is treated as a field API name on the first matching record and supports dotted paths (`{!Log__r.Name}`). Unknown tokens resolve to an empty string; a blank / all-blank template falls back to the default subject.
### Daily limits
Salesforce enforces org-wide daily email limits (5,000 external addresses/day for production, lower for trial and dev orgs). Notifications that exceed the limit fail with `Failed` status and an error message on the delivery row.
